panels.title: Service Health Status
panels.description: Green = UP, Red = DOWN. Probed every 15s via Blackbox Exporter.

panels.title: CrashLoopBackOff Pods
panels.description: Pods stuck in crash loop — needs immediate action

panels.title: ImagePullBackOff Pods
panels.description: Pods failing to pull image — deployment broken

panels.title: Nodes NotReady
panels.description: Nodes that are down or unresponsive

panels.title: Unschedulable Pods
panels.description: Pods stuck in Pending — insufficient resources or node issues

panels.title: PVC Near Full (>80%)
panels.description: Persistent volumes running out of space

panels.title: CPU Throttled Containers
panels.description: Containers being CPU throttled — app is slow even if CPU looks low

panels.title: 🚨 Problem Pods — CrashLoop / ImagePull / Pending / OOMKilled
panels.description: All pods with issues right now. First thing NOC should check.

panels.title: Nodes
panels.description: Total nodes in cluster

panels.title: Running Pods
panels.description: Total running pods

panels.title: Failed Pods
panels.description: Pods in failed state

panels.title: Pending Pods
panels.description: Pods waiting to be scheduled

panels.title: Cluster CPU Usage
panels.description: Average CPU usage across all nodes

panels.title: Cluster Memory Usage
panels.description: Average memory usage across all nodes

panels.title: API Response Time
panels.description: End-to-end probe duration per service

panels.title: Probe Success History
panels.description: 1 = UP  |  0 = DOWN — shows outage windows over time

panels.title: HTTP Status Codes
panels.description: HTTP response code — 200=OK, 5xx=error

panels.title: DNS Lookup Time
panels.description: Time to resolve DNS — spikes indicate DNS issues

panels.title: SSL Cert Expiry (days)
panels.description: Days until SSL certificate expires — alert if < 30

panels.title: Pod CPU Usage  (% of Request)
panels.description: CPU usage as % of request — same calculation HPA uses. Yellow=70% (HPA CPU threshold), Red=100%.

targets.expr: sum(rate(container_cpu_usage_seconds_total{container!="", container!="POD", namespace=~"$namespace", pod=~"$pod"}[5m])) by (namespace, pod) / on(namespace, pod) sum(kube_pod_container_resource_requests{resource="cpu", namespace=~"$namespace", pod=~"$pod"}) by (namespace, pod) * 100

panels.title: Pod Memory Usage  (% of Request)
panels.description: Memory usage as % of request — same calculation HPA uses. Yellow=80% (HPA memory threshold), Red=100%.

targets.expr: sum(container_memory_working_set_bytes{container!="", container!="POD", namespace=~"$namespace", pod=~"$pod"}) by (namespace, pod) / on(namespace, pod) sum(kube_pod_container_resource_requests{resource="memory", namespace=~"$namespace", pod=~"$pod"}) by (namespace, pod) * 100

panels.title: Replica Mismatch (Desired vs Ready)
panels.description: Non-zero = deployment is degraded

panels.title: HPA Current vs Max Replicas
panels.description: When current approaches max, autoscaler is at capacity

panels.title: HPA Actual Utilization % — CPU & Memory  (matches kubectl get hpa TARGETS)
panels.description: Exact utilization % HPA controller sees — same as TARGETS column in kubectl get hpa. Uses kube_horizontalpodautoscaler_status_target_metric from kube-state-metrics v2.x. CPU threshold line at 70%, Memory at 80%. When either crosses threshold = HPA fires.

panels.title: CPU Reserved % (Scheduler View)
panels.description: What the scheduler sees. High = pods may fail to schedule even if nodes look idle.

panels.title: CPU Actual Usage %
panels.description: Real CPU being consumed. Much lower than Reserved = pods are over-requesting.

panels.title: Memory Reserved % (Scheduler View)
panels.description: What the scheduler sees for memory.

panels.title: Memory Actual Usage %
panels.description: Real memory being consumed.

panels.title: CPU — Reserved vs Actual Usage per Node
panels.description: Gap between lines = wasted reserved capacity

panels.title: Memory — Reserved vs Actual Usage per Node
panels.description: Gap between lines = wasted reserved capacity

panels.title: Pod Resource Requests vs Actual Usage — Identify Over-Provisioned Pods
panels.description: Pods where Request >> Usage are wasting node capacity and causing scheduling failures

targets.expr: sum(rate(container_cpu_usage_seconds_total{container!="", container!="POD", container!="POD", namespace=~"$namespace", pod=~"$pod"}[5m])) by (namespace, pod)
sum(container_memory_working_set_bytes{container!="", container!="POD", container!="POD", namespace=~"$namespace", pod=~"$pod"}) by (namespace, pod)

panels.title: PVCs Near Full (>80%)
panels.description: PVCs consuming more than 80% capacity

panels.title: PVCs Critical (>95%)
panels.description: PVCs about to run out — immediate action needed

panels.title: Total PVC Capacity
panels.description: Total provisioned PVC storage across cluster

panels.title: Total PVC Used
panels.description: Total used PVC storage across cluster

panels.title: PVC Usage % per Volume
panels.description: Usage percentage per persistent volume — watch for anything approaching 100%

panels.title: PVC Available Bytes
panels.description: Remaining free space per volume

panels.title: PVC Usage Details
panels.description: Full breakdown of all PVCs — capacity, used, available, usage %

panels.title: Privileged Containers
panels.description: Containers running with privileged=true — security risk

targets.expr: count(kube_pod_container_info) * on(pod, namespace) group_left() (kube_pod_spec_containers_security_context_privileged == 1) or vector(0)

panels.title: Containers Running as Root
panels.description: Containers with runAsUser=0 — violates least privilege

panels.title: Pods Without Resource Limits
panels.description: Pods missing CPU or memory limits — can starve other pods

panels.title: Pods Without Resource Requests
panels.description: Pods missing resource requests — scheduler cannot plan properly

panels.title: Pods Without Memory Limits — Full List
panels.description: These pods can consume unlimited memory and cause OOM on the node

panels.title: Pods Without CPU Limits — Full List
panels.description: These pods can consume unlimited CPU and throttle other workloads

panels.title: Pod Restart Rate (all namespaces)
panels.description: Pods restarting right now — spikes = active incidents

panels.title: Warning Events Rate
panels.description: Rate of Kubernetes warning events — spikes indicate cluster problems

panels.title: ⚠️ Recent Kubernetes Warning Events
panels.description: Live warning events from the cluster — BackOff, Failed, Unhealthy, Evicted etc.

panels.title: Pod Evictions
panels.description: Pods evicted due to resource pressure — indicates node is under stress

panels.title: Max Replicas Reached (time range)
panels.description: Highest replica count seen across all HPAs in selected time range

panels.title: HPAs at Max Capacity Right Now
panels.description: HPAs where current replicas = max replicas — autoscaler is at ceiling

panels.title: Scale-Up Events (time range)
panels.description: Number of times replicas increased (scale-up only). Each count = HPA added pods.

panels.title: Pods Launched at Peak
panels.description: Max pods running at any point in selected time range

panels.title: HPA Replica Count Over Time — All Deployments
panels.description: Shows exactly when each deployment scaled up or down. Peak = traffic spike. Drop = scale-down after quiet period.

panels.title: Pod CPU Usage During Scaling Window  (% of Request)
panels.description: CPU % of request during scaling window. Crosses 70% = HPA CPU trigger.

targets.expr: sum(rate(container_cpu_usage_seconds_total{container!="", container!="POD", namespace=~"$namespace", pod=~"$pod"}[5m])) by (namespace, pod) / on(namespace, pod) sum(kube_pod_container_resource_requests{resource="cpu", namespace=~"$namespace", pod=~"$pod"}) by (namespace, pod) * 100

panels.title: Pod Memory Usage During Scaling Window  (% of Request)
panels.description: Memory % of request during scaling window. Crosses 80% = HPA memory trigger. This is likely what triggered your HPA.

targets.expr: sum(container_memory_working_set_bytes{container!="", container!="POD", namespace=~"$namespace", pod=~"$pod"}) by (namespace, pod) / on(namespace, pod) sum(kube_pod_container_resource_requests{resource="memory", namespace=~"$namespace", pod=~"$pod"}) by (namespace, pod) * 100

panels.title: HPA Replica Utilization % Over Time  (Current / Max Replicas)
panels.description: How close each HPA is to its max replica count. 100% = autoscaler at ceiling, needs higher maxReplicas.

panels.title: Running Pod Count Over Time
panels.description: Total pods running per deployment over time. Flat line = no scaling. Steps up/down = HPA events.

panels.title: Peak Resource Usage per Deployment — Selected Time Range
panels.description: Max CPU and memory seen per pod during the selected time window. Use this to set correct resource requests.

targets.expr: max_over_time(sum(rate(container_cpu_usage_seconds_total{container!="", container!="POD", container!="POD", namespace=~"$namespace", pod=~"$pod"}[5m])) by (namespace, pod)[$__range:])
max_over_time(sum(container_memory_working_set_bytes{container!="", container!="POD", container!="POD", namespace=~"$namespace", pod=~"$pod"}) by (namespace, pod)[$__range:])
